perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence on the Effective Date and conclude on October 31, 2010, unless renewed by agreement or sooner terminated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence on the Effective Date and conclude on October 31, 2010. The term can be extended indefinitely at Torvec's sole discretion, or sooner terminated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by allowing Torvec to unilaterally extend the agreement indefinitely, contradicting the initial defined term which concluded on October 31, 2010, creating an in-text contradiction. This affects enforcement because the 'Term' definition now has two conflicting possible end dates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence on the Effective Date and conclude on October 31, 2010. The term can be extended indefinitely at Torvec's sole discretion, or sooner terminated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating that the agreement concludes on a specific date (October 31, 2010) but then allows Torvec to extend the term indefinitely at its sole discretion. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text (both are 'The term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence on the Effective Date and conclude on October 31, 2010. The term can be extended indefinitely at Torvec's sole discretion, or sooner terminated in accordance with this Agreement.') which is the same place the contradiction is created. This directly contradicts the defined term and creates an in-text contradiction because it introduces two conflicting end dates for the agreement, which falls under the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' due to key terms being inconsistently defined. The initial, fixed term is now directly undermined by the possibility of indefinite extension, making the 'Term' definition unclear and thus, unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Torvec will provide to Vendor the use of up to 2 Differential units to be used in the T-1 C5 Corvette for testing, evaluation and racing purposes..
perturbation.changed_text: Torvec will provide to Vendor the use of up to 2 Differential units to be used in the T-1 C5 Corvette for testing, evaluation and racing purposes. These units remain the property of Vendor, despite their use by Torvec.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation contradicts Section 5, which implies that what Torvec provides is consideration for services, not a transfer of property. This creates an in-text contradiction on who owns the 'Differential units'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment and Other Consideration As consideration for the benefits it receives under this Agreement, Torvec shall provide the following to Vendor: See Exhibit A The schedule set forth in Exhibit A will constitute invoicing for the events. Notwithstanding the above, Torvec may, in its sole discretion, cancel its participation in any of the above-referenced events by notifying Vendor of such cancellation not later than two weeks prior to the due date(s) for payment hereunder. All checks shall be made payabe to: Phoenix Performance, LLC and mailed to 481, Schuylkill Rd, Phoenixville, Pa. 19460. (b) Equipment 3. Responsibilities and Benefits Vendor shall be responsible for the following in 2010: 4. Torvec Benefits During the Term of this Agreement, Torvec shall be entitled to the following sponsorship benefits: a) Primary sponsorship rights to all of Vendor's participation efforts in the above race events. b) The right to specify and approve all team sponsorship identification markings, logos, graphics, etc. for vehicle livery, team equipment and uniforms. c) Sole right to disapprove any driver selected by Vendor for any reason. The driver for these events will be John Heinricy. (a) Payment Schedule Torvec will provide to Vendor the use of up to 2 Differential units to be used in the T-1 C5 Corvette for testing, evaluation and racing purposes. These units remain the property of Vendor, despite their use by Torvec..
perturbation.justification: The original text implicitly suggests that Torvec is providing the differential units as consideration. The changed text explicitly states that the units remain the property of the Vendor, despite being used by Torvec. This contradicts the original implication that Torvec transfers ownership, creating an in-text contradiction as it relates to consideration described in Section 5 and thus a structural flaw that is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may change its address for notice by giving written notice to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may change its address for notice by giving verbal notice to the other party. Written notice is permissible but not required.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the required form of notice, creating a contradiction. Section 10 specifies that 'All notices provided herein shall be in writing', while the change allows for verbal notice. This creates an in-text contradiction of whether a written notice is required or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All notices provided herein shall be in writing and are effective upon receipt if hand delivered, sent by overnight courier (with ability to confirm receipt), by fax or by registered or certified mail, return receipt requested, addressed to the respective parties hereto as follows: Either party may change its address for notice by giving verbal notice to the other party. Written notice is permissible but not required.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that address changes must be communicated via written notice. The changed text allows for verbal notice. This directly contradicts the section requiring all notices to be in writing, as the changed text permits a form of notice (verbal) that the contradicted text explicitly prohibits. This creates a clear inconsistency in the enforceability of notice requirements, fitting the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'. It also introduces ambiguity where the contradicted clause expects certainty.
